problem_statement: At the Monday 05:50 planning cycle, a regional meal service assigned allergen-controlled totes for Riverbend Residence to the loading stop for Riverbend Rehabilitation, while the printed recipient labels remained correct.

observations.statement: The first misrouting appeared in the 05:50 plan generated ten minutes after the weekly facility roster was accepted; the preceding 04:50 plan used the prior roster and routed both Riverbend facilities correctly.
observations.provenance: Dispatch audit timeline reconciled with roster-ingest receipts by the morning incident lead.

observations.statement: The retained roster snapshot contains two active rows with facility key RB-044: the long-standing Rehabilitation row and a newly added Residence row. Their names, addresses, and dietary programs are otherwise distinct.
observations.provenance: Read-only copy of the exact signed roster object consumed at 05:40.

observations.statement: The retained planner index contains one RB-044 destination whose address is the later Residence row, while the route-stop table still associates RB-044 with the established Rehabilitation loading stop.
observations.provenance: Planner index and route-stop export captured before operators made corrections.

observations.statement: Handheld scan logs independently show all 18 affected totes carried the correct Residence name and allergen profile but resolved to stop RB-044; no tote label was regenerated between packing and dispatch.
observations.provenance: Immutable scanner event export from three loading-lane devices.

observations.statement: An isolated replay using the same planner artifact, route table, and settings produced correct routes with the prior roster and the observed misroutes with the 05:40 roster.
observations.provenance: Two-run replay performed against copied inputs with outbound dispatch disabled.

observations.statement: Changing only the new Residence row to the unused key RB-071 in a copied roster restored all 18 destinations and produced no route changes for the other 312 totes.
observations.provenance: Read-only shadow-plan comparison approved by meal-safety operations.

observations.statement: Planner artifact hashes, route rules, feature settings, and host image identifiers match the prior seven successful planning cycles.
observations.provenance: Release attestation and host inventory exported by separate platform automation.

observations.statement: All 41 facilities whose active roster keys were unique retained their established loading stops, including six facilities added through the same intake workflow during the previous month.
observations.provenance: Route-delta report cross-checked by dispatch quality staff.
